name: ICU Patient Monitoring State Diagram with Fork-Join Parallel Regions
input: Create a state diagram for ICU patient monitoring with parallel state regions. Initial state: Patient Admitted to ICU. Then a fork into 3 parallel monitoring regions that run concurrently: Region 1 — Cardiac Monitoring: Normal Sinus Rhythm, Tachycardia, Bradycardia, Arrhythmia Detected, Code Blue. Region 2 — Respiratory Monitoring: Spontaneous Breathing, Ventilator Assisted, Weaning Trial, Extubation Ready. Region 3 — Neurological Monitoring: Alert and Oriented, Sedated, Decreased Consciousness, Unresponsive. All 3 regions join when patient meets discharge criteria -> Stable for Transfer -> Transferred to Floor (terminal). If any region enters critical state (Code Blue, Unresponsive) -> Emergency Intervention state that overrides all regions. Show the parallel/concurrent regions using fork and join notation.
qualityCriteria: Cardiac monitoring region with at least 3 cardiac states
Respiratory monitoring region with ventilator and weaning states
Neurological monitoring region with consciousness levels
Fork notation for parallel region entry
Join notation for discharge criteria convergence
Terminal state (Transferred to Floor or similar)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: stateDiagram-v2 with fork/join, 3 parallel monitoring regions (cardiac, respiratory, neuro), emergency override, 16+ states.
